title: rosa canina var. micropetala
intro: Rosa canina var. micropetala, also known as the Dwarf Wild Rose or Small-flowered Dog Rose, is a variety of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are found in its hips, also known as rose hips. The hips of the Rosa canina var. micropetala plant have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as colds, fever, and digestive problems, due to its high content of vitamins A and C, as well as potassium and other essential minerals. Rosa canina var. micropetala can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
